summary: The student asked for the difference between RTO and RPO. The assistant explained that RTO (Recovery Time Objective) is the maximum acceptable downtime after a disaster, focusing on how quickly operations need to be restored. RPO (Recovery Point Objective) is the maximum acceptable data loss, measured in time, focusing on how current the data needs to be. The assistant provided an analogy of a flight to illustrate the difference, with RTO being the maximum wait time before departure and RPO being the maximum time before the flight is considered too late.
